content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** positive integers.

Find the **smallest** positive integer ddd such that there exists **no** pair of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N)(1\le i\lt j \le N)(1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N) where abs(Ai‚ąíAj)abs(A_i - A_j)abs(Ai‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč) is divisible by ddd.

In other words, d‚ą§abs(Ai‚ąíAj)d \nmid abs(A_i - A_j)d‚ą§abs(Ai‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč) for all (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N)(1\le i \lt j\le N)(1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integers NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated distinct integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots , A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the smallest positive integer ddd such that d‚ą§(Ai‚ąíAj)d \nmid (A_i - A_j)d‚ą§(Ai‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč) for all (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N)(1\le i \lt j\le N)(1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N).
constraints: The sums of NNN and max(A)max(A)max(A) over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10 ^ 53‚čÖ105.

Test case 111: The possible differences are abs(A1‚ąíA2)=1,abs(A1‚ąíA3)=2,abs(A2‚ąíA3)=1abs(A_1 - A_2) = 1, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 2, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 1abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč)=1,abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=2,abs(A2‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=1. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 333.
Test case 222: The possible differences are abs(A1‚ąíA2)=1,abs(A1‚ąíA3)=9,abs(A2‚ąíA3)=8abs(A_1 - A_2) = 1, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 9, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 8abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč)=1,abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=9,abs(A2‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=8. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 555.
Test case 333: The possible differences are abs(A1‚ąíA2)=2,abs(A1‚ąíA3)=9,abs(A2‚ąíA3)=7abs(A_1 - A_2) = 2, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 9, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 7abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč)=2,abs(A1‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=9,abs(A2‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč)=7. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 444.
